name: Pooka
description: The Pooka, also spelled Púca, Phouka, or Púka, is a shapeshifting spirit from Irish folklore. Often considered a solitary fairy, the Pooka is known for its mischievous and sometimes malevolent nature. It can take on various forms, most commonly appearing as a sleek, black horse, a goat, a dog, a cat, or even a human. In its animal form, the Pooka often possesses the power of speech. While typically associated with Halloween, the Pooka is an active force throughout the year, particularly during the darker months. Though capable of causing harm, Pookas are not inherently evil. They often enjoy playing tricks on humans, such as leading travelers astray or damaging property. However, if treated with respect, a Pooka can also offer valuable advice or even grant favors. Some tales depict them as benevolent protectors of animals and the countryside. The Pooka embodies the unpredictable nature of the wild and the complex relationship between humans and the spirit world in Irish tradition.
subtitle: A shapeshifting trickster spirit from Irish folklore.
appearance: The Pooka can appear in many forms, most commonly as a black horse with glowing yellow eyes. Other forms include a goat, dog, cat, raven, or even a tall, dark human figure. In its animal forms, it often has the power of human speech.
habitat: Rural areas of Ireland, particularly isolated fields, forests, and near ancient ruins.
behavior: The Pooka is known for its unpredictable and often mischievous behavior. It enjoys playing tricks on humans, leading travelers astray, and causing minor damage. However, it can also be helpful and offer advice if treated with respect. It is said to be most active at night and during the Halloween season.
weaknesses: Respectful treatment (can be turned into an obligation)

keyMyths.title: The Pooka's Ride
keyMyths.description: One common tale tells of a farmer who is offered a ride on the back of a Pooka in its horse form. The ride is wild and terrifying, but the Pooka ultimately delivers the farmer safely home, albeit shaken. This story highlights the Pooka's chaotic and unpredictable nature, showcasing both danger and a strange form of protection. The farmer's survival depends on holding on tight and not showing fear.

keyMyths.title: The Pooka's Advice
keyMyths.description: In some stories, a Pooka offers wise advice to those who treat it with respect. This advice can be crucial for solving problems or averting disasters. However, the Pooka's guidance is often cryptic and requires careful interpretation. The tale emphasizes the importance of respecting the natural world and the spirits that inhabit it, as well as the rewards that can come from such respect.
keyMyths.source: Lysaght, Patricia. *The Banshee: The Irish Death Messenger*.

keyMyths.title: The Pooka and the Blacksmith
keyMyths.description: A blacksmith, known for his strength and cunning, once captured a Pooka. Instead of harming it, he forced the Pooka to work for him, performing difficult tasks. The Pooka, bound by its word, begrudgingly complied. This tale shows a rare instance of a human controlling a Pooka, but also highlights the importance of fairness, as the blacksmith's actions were not motivated by malice but by need.
keyMyths.source: Wilde, Lady. *Ancient Legends, Mystic Charms, and Superstitions of Ireland*.

sources.author: Briggs, Katharine
sources.description: A comprehensive guide to fairies and other supernatural creatures in folklore, including detailed entries on the Pooka.
